chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُحْرِمِ يُقَبِّلُ امْرَأَتَهُ وَ يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهَا بِشَهْوَةٍ أَوْ غَيْرِ شَهْوَةٍ أَوْ يَنْظُرُ إِلَى غَيْرِهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 104 – The one in Ihraam kisses his wife and he looks at her with desire, or without desire, or he looks at someone else

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/356)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/356)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: الْمُحْرِمِ تُنْعَتُ لَهُ الْمَرْأَةُ الْجَمِيلَةُ الْخِلْقَةِ فَيُمْنِي قَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْ‏ءٌ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Sama’at,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding the one in Ihraam to whom the beautiful woman describes her creation, so he discharges. Heasws said: ‘There is nothing upon her’.
en.sarwar: 12. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from Sama‘ah who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is in the state of Ihram, a beautiful woman describes her beauty and he discharges semen. He (the Imam) said, ‘There is nothing on him.’”
